words: This conversation with Anna Bengston Thomason and Oscar Thomason took place
in their home on Dry Ridge between Troy and Deary, Idaho on Ferbruary third,
1976. The interviewer is Sam Schrager.
SAM SCHRAGER: Where were you born in Sweden?
ANNA THOMASON: I'm so hard of hearing.

speaker: SS
words: I asked where it was you were born.

speaker: AT
words: Yeah, I was just born where I was raised after that in wen we called the

speaker: AT
words: place and it was close to Torsby in Varmland.

speaker: SS
words: And you were born just a few milea from there?

speaker: SS
words: OSCAR THOMASON: Nein, from there I was born six and a half Swedish miles. More north.

speaker: SS
words: One Swedish mile from the Norwegian border. And they come in from Finland.

speaker: SS
words: 1640. And they settled all over, up in the hills, both sides of the border.

speaker: SS
words: It was 1640 they went in.

speaker: SS
words: They were from Finland before?

speaker: OT
words: Yeah, they come right from Finland.

speaker: SS
words: They were Finns and not Swedish!

speaker: OT
words: They were mixed probably some were Swedish Finns. You see, Sweden and Finland

speaker: OT
words: belongs to Sweden I think for around 600 years. In 1917,'18 after the

speaker: OT
words: the first World War, the Finn give away, Russia took it from Sweden. And

speaker: OT
words: in 1918 and '18 the Finnish people get away from Russia. Then Russia had to

speaker: OT
words: give 'em independent. There was in kind of one way a revolution.

speaker: SS
words: Do you think that your ancestors had come from Finland?

speaker: OT
words: Yeah, from inland. On my dad's side they come. But where I come from is all

speaker: OT
words: old Finnish names.

speaker: SS
words: Is that true for Anna's place too?

speaker: OT
words: No, there was no, that far down. No, that was more south.

speaker: SS

speaker: OT
words: They get along wonderful. All of the was Finn, they nearly all come from

speaker: OT
words: Finland, see. And they was all up in the hills, you know, and they could cut

speaker: OT
words: down trees. And they burned trees in the fall, then they seeded rye. You

speaker: OT
words: know what rye is. They seeded that, you see. And they build up in the woods

speaker: OT
words: you know, things like that. Then later on you know, the company, they don't

speaker: OT
words: like to see that done that, so they took the land back but they could live there

speaker: OT
words: and use it if they,, and then they have to pay little tax,

speaker: SS
words: Who was the company?

speaker: OT
words: There was, they called, the company was, they called it Isvala Bulog, Bulog

speaker: OT
words: is the same as the "company," see.

speaker: SS
words: Was it owned by rich Swedish people?

speaker: OT
words: Yeah, but it was a company, see, Like now we can call it Potlatch Lumber

speaker: OT
words: Companies.

speaker: SS
words: Same kind of things.

speaker: OT
words: Yeah. What it was see. And yau have to pay a little bit of tax, but there

speaker: OT
words: wasn't very much.

speaker: SS
words: When you grew up there, was your family pretty poor? They didn't have much

speaker: SS
words: money did they?

speaker: OT
words: No. There wasn't much money. And they worked, they growed up and my dad, he

speaker: OT
words: worked for the company,see. And they don't pay very much There wasn't too

speaker: OT
words: much to go on, see.

speaker: SS
words: Was that the same, most of the people were poor there?

speaker: OT
words: There was some. There was some that owned the land and they owned lot of

speaker: OT
words: timber, see. Timber land. The company bought them out, see. Gave them.'pretty

words: good price and there was no price hardly on the timber. So they bought it

speaker: OT
words: pretty cheap. After the company get it, then look out, the price went up,

speaker: AT
words: Wasn't that way. Was good for some of the, that didn't have a home because

speaker: AT
words: the company used to have places for them to stay,

speaker: OT
words: Well they had the company build houses for them you see,

speaker: SS
words: Sounds like here with Potlatch. They bought the timber from the people,

speaker: OT
words: Yeah. Did right. They bought the timber from the people,

speaker: AT
words: So that helped them that they had a place to live,

speaker: OT
words: And the tax they payed were very little. And it was poor land, lot of rocks.

speaker: SS
words: So there was no farming to speal of there.

speaker: OT
words: No, there wasn't.

speaker: AT

words: was made up(both talk at onece.) It was covered with a little dirt, but, ho

speaker: SS

speaker: AT
words: Yeah.

speaker: OT
words: On the county road, they were kind of big rock in them,we used to see 'em

speaker: OT
words: and they, what we call the highway, only they call it lundsvag.Then there

speaker: OT
words: were big rock that...Now they have much better road. Little wider too.

speaker: AT

words: to some friends and I thought I'd stumble and stumble on those roads. Those

speaker: AT
words: rocks, they weren't big, but they were kind of, the road wasn't smooth. That's

speaker: AT
words: what I know about.

speaker: OT
words: You don't remember too, but you lived out the west, they call it, there was

speaker: OT
words: rock in that road. Big big rocks, see. And they drove with one horse and a

speaker: OT
words: wagon. And you hit the rock with your horse,

speaker: AT
words: So it was, they had highway that come not far from where he lived, it wasn't

speaker: AT
words: far.

words: Was there farming where your family lived?

speaker: AT
words: It was farming. I don't say we had so big place, but it was farming there.

speaker: SS

speaker: OT
words: You can call it, probably 20 acres. And it was pretty good land and it was

speaker: OT
words: level, see. And in those days there were some real big farmers,

words: We had a neighbor, what they call it, they had lots of land and on the other

speaker: AT
words: side of us...

speaker: OT
words: On the other side of the highway, you know, against the big lake, there was

speaker: OT
words: big farmers.

words: bit of land?

speaker: AT
words: He build houses.

speaker: OT
words: Carpenter.

words: raised pigs and things like that. Had their chickens but...

words: He couldn't make living.

speaker: AT
words: No, we were so many kids too.

speaker: AT

speaker: AT
words: youngest was born, so we weren't eight at one time. So, no, my dad was out

speaker: AT
words: working.

words: When you were growing np as achild, what was the honse like?Did you have

speaker: AT
words: some.

speaker: OT
words: Good house, they had a good house.

speaker: SS

speaker: AT
words: No. I'm sure papa didn't build that,

speaker: OT
words: No.

speaker: AT
words: But Iwas born and raised there. My oldest brother, how old was Jalmar?

speaker: AT
words: Werner was born there, the first one, that was the third in the family

speaker: AT
words: down. He was born there where we live. So and after that, all the rest of

speaker: AT
words: us, of course.

speaker: SS
words: Jalmar came to the U,S. when he was just a young man?

speaker: AT
words: Most of my brothers went here when they were young. They weren't very old.

speaker: OT
words: Jalmar came here in 1905.

speaker: AT
words: He come here, my mother had abrother in Idaho and he went there. And

speaker: AT
words: then after one went, then it was easy for the other to go. So.

words: it was like here or did he just find out from letters?

speaker: AT
words: I didn't know, I was quite young. But he was back there, oh yes,

speaker: OT
words: He asked your mother's brother if he come back to Sweden and you know your

speaker: OT
words: Uncle Sven come back.

speaker: AT
words: Yeah, they was my mother's brother,

speaker: OT
words: He was asking if he said something about America around here, how it was

speaker: OT
words: here.

speaker: AT
words: Maybe he did.

speaker: SS
words: I'm just wondering how Jalmar decided that he should come here

speaker: AT
words: America was Jalmar's(home) so he thought a lot of Anerica,

speaker: OT
words: How did he find out if he never went here?

speaker: AT
words: He find put before you come here from Jalmar how things were here. He got

speaker: AT
words: you started,

speaker: OT
words: His uncle was here with us, you see,

speaker: SS
words: What did they say about Aperica?

speaker: AT
words: That I can't explain exactly. There was things he told, he felt that were good,

speaker: AT
words: about Anerica, I can't say anything else because. It was too bad he culdn't

speaker: AT
words: come back, because he sure thought a lot of America, And Troy,

speaker: SS
words: Jalmar couldn't come back, why

speaker: AT
words: His wife didn't like, he married, one time when he was home. He married and

speaker: AT
words: she didn't want to come. And he come back here, because he thogut maybe

speaker: AT
words: she will come, he even had the ticket and all, but she didn't come either

speaker: AT
words: so it was just the same for papa as it was for Jalmar, And both of my

speaker: AT
words: brothers and my dad went home when the wife didn't want to come,

speaker: SS
words: He even bought her a ticket?

speaker: AT
words: yes, they said papa and I never discussed that, But I heard that said, because

speaker: AT
words: that was long before I was here, born. But they said that he bought the

speaker: AT
words: ticket, but she didn't want to come. So,

speaker: SS
words: Why do you think women wouldn't want to leave Sweden, why would women want

speaker: SS
words: to stay?

speaker: AT

speaker: OT
words: They were afraid of the water.

speaker: SS
words: What?

speaker: OT
words: They were afraid for the water. To go over the ocean. Go on the boat,

speaker: AT
words: I don't know.

speaker: OT
words: I think your mother was,

speaker: AT
words: Yal I think so, because that was a long time ago,

speaker: OT
words: It took a long time,

speaker: AT
words: To go on the ocean,

speaker: OT
words: You had to go on the boat,

speaker: SS
words: You think maybe they didn't want to give up their friends?

speaker: AT
words: Oh yeah. And like for Jalmar's wife, it was her parents was getting old. So

speaker: AT
words: that had to do with it too. Because she had a brother, though, that stayed

speaker: AT
words: home with mother, mama and daddy. But I don't know if Marie ever want to go

speaker: AT
words: anyway.

speaker: SS
words: It must have made it hard for families to be separated.

speaker: AT
words: I tried it, so I know how it is. He went ahead of me. But for me to go to

speaker: AT
words: America, that was different, because I had so many of my family,

speaker: OT
words: You had so many brothers that went over, so that was kind of hard for your

speaker: OT
words: mother and even your dad, they had left, all of them, you see,

speaker: AT
words: One after another left. Dad, never that I know, talked to the boys, that

speaker: AT
words: were the first ones, that they shouldn't go, I don't know why, He took

speaker: AT
words: everyone to Torsby,to put them on the train there and that was something he

speaker: AT
words: did. But he was really a thinking man too, so I guess maybe he saw it would

speaker: AT
words: be better for them,

speaker: SS

speaker: AT
words: Ya. Oh yes. He was in Sweden and he worked hard too, Of course, when he

speaker: AT
words: was here in America it wasn't cars and things so he couldn't tell them

speaker: AT
words: anything about that either, 1 don't know it if ever was a car

speaker: AT
words: Oh of course long, long time ago, I heard it said that you cut gold with

speaker: AT
words: a knife and (laughs)it wasn't any bad things.

speaker: OT

speaker: AT

speaker: OT

speaker: AT
words: It was bad.

speaker: OT
words: And they were talking about that, you see,

speaker: AT

speaker: AT

words: of course, then they write "Wild West" sometime to be fun. Of course, papa

speaker: AT
words: was here, and I think he would have rather seen the kids come here.

speaker: SS

speaker: AT

speaker: OT

speaker: AT

speaker: AT

speaker: AT
words: to come home.

speaker: AT

speaker: AT
words: they know where papa had had a lot. In Seattle. He bought that, but then

speaker: AT

speaker: AT
words: my brothers know where it was. Just about middle of Seattle, So you see, that

speaker: AT
words: was...But he worked and was...

speaker: AT

speaker: AT
words: not those six weeks, I had left,

words: were two at least in the hired in there. So,

words: I don't know why, because he was, is kind of worried for him because he was

words: more thin. He's alright, he lives in Seattle and he's alright. But we can

words: talk about things. Oh yes. No, and the boys and I all those boys, but we

words: me and things lifee that,

words: being the fifth one that way. And I'm in the middle,the fourth,
